---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# nycsets <img src="man/figures/nycsets.png" align="right" width="360">

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

`nycsets` packages film, television, and other production permits issued by
the New York City Mayor's Office of Media and Entertainment (MOME). It
provides one main permits table and three long companion tables that link
each permit to its affected community boards, NYPD precincts, and ZIP codes.

## Installation

You can install the development version of nycsets from GitHub with:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("kjhealy/nycsets")
```

## Load

```{r load, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(nycsets)
```

## What's included

```{r included}
nyc_film_permits_df

nyc_film_permit_boards_df

nyc_film_permit_precincts_df

nyc_film_permit_zips_df
```

## Examples

Top production categories by borough:

```{r borough-category}
nyc_film_permits_df |>
  filter(!is.na(borough)) |>
  count(borough, category) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = borough,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(Manhattan))
```

Permits per day over time:

```{r permits-by-day, fig.alt = "Line chart of daily NYC film permit counts from 2023 through early 2026, showing weekly cycles and seasonal variation."}
nyc_film_permits_df |>
  filter(!is.na(start_date_time)) |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(start_date_time)) |>
  count(date) |>
  ggplot(aes(date, n)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Permits issued",
    title = "NYC film permits per day",
    caption = "Source: NYC MOME via NYC OpenData"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Top ten ZIP codes by permit count:

```{r top-zips}
nyc_film_permit_zips_df |>
  count(zip_code, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)
```
